headline: Educators converge on production agents: orchestration loops, security, and observability now define the agentic stack.
body: The recommended channels center on moving agents into production rather than prompting them by hand: Cole Medin reports that top engineers now run automated loops where an orchestrator dispatches parallel worker agents, trading manual intervention for higher token costs and a need for observability harnesses [1], while Cyril Imhof frames the same harness engineering and loop pattern against regulated industries and open versus closed model risk [4]. IBM Technology supplies the deployment and security spine: agents coordinated through workflows and policy enforcement with human oversight [0], a debate over whether agents should manage passwords amid patch management and C-suite risk tolerance [3], and Kagenti's identity-based, zero-trust delegation chains for multi-agent systems [5]. Outside the inner circle the same themes recur concretely: Databricks lays out a five-pillar production framework (evaluation, observability, data foundation, orchestration, governance) grounded in a bank chatbot that caught 47 PII breaches via tracing [8], and Model Context Protocol anchors hands-on builds spanning tool connections, LangGraph travel agents with PostgreSQL memory, and a Gemini vision agent [7][9][13]. AI Jason adds a 30-plus hour loop-building template and course [6], and Better Stack reviews Voicebox, a local-first open-source ElevenLabs alternative with voice cloning and MCP support [2].
